commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=953cfe2465e259894db77cd357446c96af062bb0)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds shared aborted-stop-reason handling across lifecycle wait snapshots, subagent completion, announcement fallback, and regression tests so aborted subagent runs become terminated errors.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level with high confidence. Current main classifies lifecycle `end` snapshots and subagent listener completions with `stopReason: "aborted"` as successful unless a separate `aborted: true` or blocked liveness signal is present.
commands.review_followup: Not queued for automated repair because external-PR real behavior proof is still a contributor merge gate, even though the remaining dedupe normalization is mechanically clear.

target.title: fix(agents): treat aborted subagent runs as terminal [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `agent.wait` and subagent completion handling could treat lifecycle `end` events with `stopReason: "aborted"` as successful `ok` completions when `aborted: true` was absent.
- Solution: Normalize the canonical aborted stop reason into an error/terminated outcome before wait snapshots and subagent announcements can mark the run complete.
- What changed: Added a shared aborted-stop-reason helper, mapped gateway wait snapshots to `status: "error"`, and taught the subagent run manager, direct lifecycle listener, and announcement fallback to report aborted runs as killed/terminated failures.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No provider, channel, plugin API, config-shape, timeout, or fallback behavior changes.

## Motivation

Issue #72293 is a P1 session-state/message-loss bug. An aborted subagent run should not look successful to the parent session, because that can silently hide an interrupted child run and produce the wrong parent-facing completion state during the beta.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #72293
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: terminal lifecycle events with `stopReason: "aborted"` no longer produce successful `agent.wait`/subagent completion outcomes.
- Real environment tested: disposable Linux OpenClaw checkout, Node 22.22.2, pnpm 11.2.2.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts \
  src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts \
  src/agents/run-wait.test.ts
```

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts
```

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-announce.format.e2e.test.ts
```

- Evidence after fix (copied terminal output):

```text
10 test files passed / 271 tests passed
4 test files passed / 76 tests passed
1 test file passed / 79 tests passed
```

- Observed result after fix: aborted wait snapshots become `status: "error"` with `stopReason: "aborted"` preserved; subagent wait/lifecycle completions announce `subagent run terminated` and store `endedReason: "subagent-killed"` instead of completing as success.
- What was not tested: no external provider/channel account was used; this issue is in the local gateway/agent/subagent lifecycle path.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the added regressions encode the failing shape from #72293: `status: "ok"` plus `stopReason: "aborted"` previously flowed through as success.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: wait/completion code distinguished timeout-style `aborted: true` from ordinary successful lifecycle ends, but did not also treat the canonical terminal `stopReason: "aborted"` as non-success.
- Missing detection / guardrail: regression coverage existed for killed persisted session state, but not for raw wait/lifecycle terminal events that carried only `stopReason: "aborted"`.
- Contributing context (if known): the session lifecycle persistence path already maps the same terminal stop reason to killed, so the bug was a cross-layer normalization gap.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/run-wait.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/subagent-announce.format.e2e.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `stopReason: "aborted"` cannot pass through as an `ok` wait/subagent completion.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it covers each boundary where the bad successful state could enter or be announced: lifecycle emission, gateway wait snapshot, wait normalization, registry completion, announcement fallback, and parent announcement formatting.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): session lifecycle state already covered persisted killed mapping, but not the live wait/subagent completion path.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Aborted subagent runs are now reported to the parent as terminated failures instead of successful completions.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
lifecycle end stopReason=aborted -> agent.wait ok -> subagent complete -> parent sees success

After:
lifecycle end stopReason=aborted -> agent.wait error -> subagent-killed -> parent sees terminated failure
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux disposable checkout
- Runtime/container: Node 22.22.2, pnpm 11.2.2
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Emit or return a lifecycle/wait terminal snapshot with `status: "ok"` and `stopReason: "aborted"`.
2. Let `agent.wait` and the subagent registry process the terminal state.
3. Observe the parent-facing subagent outcome.

### Expected

- The run is treated as a terminated failure, with `stopReason: "aborted"` preserved and subagent `endedReason: "subagent-killed"`.

### Actual

- After this patch, matches expected.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Additional verification:

```text
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
passed

node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 <11 touched files>
passed

node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json <11 touched files>
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.

git diff --check
passed
```

`corepack pnpm check:changed --timed` passed all guards plus core/core-test typechecks, then the testserver killed full `lint core` in `tsgolint` with `SIGKILL`. A targeted lint of the 11 touched files passed with 0 warnings / 0 errors. Crabbox could not run on the testserver because the installed `crabbox` binary failed its basic sanity check before code checks started.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: aborted lifecycle end emitted from embedded terminal state; gateway wait snapshot mapping; raw wait normalization; subagent wait completion; direct lifecycle completion; announcement fallback; parent announcement E2E formatting.
- Edge cases checked: existing timeout `aborted: true` behavior remains timeout; yielded runs still pause; blocked liveness remains an error; recoverable wait errors still retry unless the stop reason is aborted.
- What you did **not** verify: live external provider/channel traffic.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: treating every `stopReason: "aborted"` as non-success could affect callers that previously relied on silent success.
  - Mitigation: this matches existing persisted lifecycle semantics for killed runs and only targets the canonical aborted terminal reason.
target.author: iwhatsskill
